questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: Its anhydrous form is an ionic compound
It exists as a dimer in non polar solvents like benzene
The dimeric structure disappears when dissolved in water
It shows back bonding of lone pairs of chlorine to the vacant p-orbital of aluminium
questions.solution:
Answer (B, C)
Anhydrous $\mathrm{AlCl}_3$ is covalent and it does not show back bonding.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The correct statement(s) about $\mathrm{B}_2 \mathrm{H}_6$ is / are :
options: There are two types of $\mathrm{H}$ present
questions.solution: In diborane, all boron atoms are $\mathrm{sp}^3$ hybridised. It is diamagnetic and contains $3 \mathrm{C}-2 \mathrm{e}$ bonds. There are two types of hydrogens $-$ terminal and bridging.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Consider the silicates and their formula given and select the one(s) which are correctly matched with their type
questions.solution:
Answer (A, C, D)
Three-dimensional silicates have all four oxygen atoms shared.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The compounds which exhibit aromatic character are
questions.solution:
To be aromatic following condition must be
(i) Planar
(ii) Conjugation
(iii) $\quad(4 n+2) \pi$ electron $(n=0,1,2)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the given statement(s) is/are correct?
options: Silicon doesn't form $\pi$ bond with oxygen and instead it forms 3D lattice
$\mathrm{SiCl}_4$ is easily hydrolysed as compared to $\mathrm{CCl}_4$
Si has a greater tendency to catenate as compared to C
Si has a greater density as compared to graphite
questions.solution:
Answer (A, B, D)
Catenation tendency of C is greater then that of Si .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
